summary: Verified Microsoft GraphRAG docs and Microsoft Research pages for graph-based RAG vs baseline semantic snippet retrieval. Produced 6 claims with source URLs, confidence, implementation implications for DinoBrain graph/LLM Wiki/source lineage, and caveats. Read-only with no source file edits.
decisions: Use official Microsoft GraphRAG docs and Microsoft Research posts as primary evidence.

Frame graph retrieval as an added retrieval layer for cross-document synthesis, holistic themes, entity-neighborhood queries, and source lineage, not as a blanket replacement for vector/snippet retrieval.
Keep implementation implications concrete for an Obsidian-like graph plus LLM Wiki/source lineage.
next_steps: Consider a DinoBrain design note for hybrid retrieval: baseline semantic/BM25 snippets plus entity graph, relationship graph, community reports, and source TextUnit provenance.
Evaluate on DinoBrain-local question classes: entity-specific, whole-vault themes, cross-note synthesis, and audit/provenance lookup.
search_queries: Microsoft GraphRAG docs indexing dataflow outputs query global local DRIFT
Microsoft Research GraphRAG baseline RAG provenance DRIFT Search
